name: Pingdom API
what_it_does: Website and API uptime monitoring service with REST API for managing checks, retrieving uptime metrics, querying performance data, and managing alerting contacts and integrations.
use_cases: Querying uptime and response time metrics for SLA reporting automation
Creating and managing synthetic HTTP/HTTPS/TCP/DNS monitors programmatically
Triggering incident response workflows based on Pingdom downtime webhooks
Aggregating multi-site performance data for status page automation
Managing on-call alert contacts and escalation policies via API
not_for: Application performance monitoring with code-level tracing (use Datadog or New Relic)
Real User Monitoring (RUM) — Pingdom is synthetic monitoring only
best_when: An agent monitors external website and API availability from multiple global locations and needs to feed uptime data into incident management or reporting systems.
avoid_when: You need full-stack APM — Pingdom is external synthetic monitoring only.

auth.notes: API token from Pingdom account settings. Bearer token in Authorization header. Account-level token with full access — no scope granularity. Organization accounts support multiple users.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $15/month (10 uptime checks); Professional from $49/month; Enterprise on request
pricing.notes: SolarWinds-owned Pingdom. Pricing based on number of checks and check frequency. API access included in all plans. Additional RUM users priced separately.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; reasonable usage limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec and Swagger documentation available. REST API covers all check management and results retrieval. Error responses are JSON with HTTP status codes. Webhook delivery for real-time downtime events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Check creation by name is not idempotent — duplicate checks can be created. Check updates by ID are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Check ID required for all operations — must query list to find check IDs by name
Historical result data has retention limits based on plan tier
Downtime events are delivered via webhook in near-real-time — polling has delay
Multiple probe locations report independently — aggregate analysis required for global uptime

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 and GDPR compliant. TLS enforced. Single account-level token without scopes is a limitation. SolarWinds security practices apply (note: 2020 SolarWinds breach was in separate product).
